title: Sponsorship FilterSet (report_type = 8)
description: Schema for the FilterSet payload of CAMPAIGN_MANAGEMENT (8) reports — sponsorship deal tracking. Field names and types mirror dashboard.models.FilterSet 1:1 — no invented fields. Phase 2 emits a JSON object that conforms to this schema.

_tl_source_of_truth.report_type_meaning: CAMPAIGN_MANAGEMENT — sponsorship/AdLink-level reporting. Filters are evaluated against thoughtleaders_adlink (joined to Brand and Channel).
_tl_source_of_truth.note: Same Django model as the intelligence schema, but only a subset of fields is meaningful for sponsorship reporting. Fields not listed here exist on the model but are inert for type 8.

channels.description: Restrict sponsorships to those involving specific channels. Resolve names → IDs via the name_resolver tool first.

brands.description: Restrict sponsorships to those involving specific brands. Resolve names → IDs via the name_resolver tool first.

days_ago.description: Rolling lower bound: only sponsorships with send_date in the last N days.

createdat_from.description: AdLink created_at lower bound — when the deal entered the pipeline (NOT the publish date).

_tl_intent_hints: Default to 'sponsored_mentions' for type 8 unless the user specifically asks about organic mentions.

tl_sponsorships_only.description: Restrict to sponsorships TL sourced/managed (vs. organic/external sponsorship signals).

sort.description: Sortable column key from references/sortable_columns.json. Format: 'field' (asc) or '-field' (desc).

filters_json.description: Catch-all JSONField the platform interprets directly. For type 8 this is the primary place to encode publish_status, deal_status, price ranges, and any sponsorship-specific signals that don't have a first-class column on FilterSet.
_tl_intent_hints: publish_status: encode the user's intent ('live', 'pending', 'sold', 'proposed') here; the platform reads it.
Deal-stage filtering (matches → proposals → deals) goes through filters_json — there is no first-class status field on FilterSet.

_tl_rules: Type 8 ALWAYS needs a date scope. If the user gives no date hint, default to days_ago=365 (one year of deal history). Unbounded sponsorship queries are meaningless and will time out.
Do NOT invent fields. Sponsorship status / publish_status / deal stage all live inside `filters_json` — encode them there, not as new top-level keys.
Resolve brand/channel names → IDs via the name_resolver tool BEFORE emitting `brands`/`channels` arrays. Type 8 with unresolved names is a hard failure mode.

Keyword fields (`keywords`, `keyword_operator`, `content_fields`, `keyword_content_fields_map`, `keyword_exclude_map`) exist on the model but are inert for type 8 — sponsorships are filtered by relations, not content text. Do not emit them.
Topic fields (`topics`) are inert for type 8 — same reason. Do not emit.
Default `channel_formats: [4]` and `languages: ['en']` unless the user explicitly contradicts them.
